# Prompts used in PrepForge

Every place the app (or its build) uses an LLM prompt is documented here — the exact text, why it
exists, and what it produces. Keep this in sync when prompts change.

## 1. Seed question-bank generation (build-time, one-off)

**Why.** The shipped `backend/content/questions.json` (100 questions) was generated by a Claude
subagent during the build, then validated. This is the prompt that produced it. Re-run it to
regenerate or extend the bank.

**Produces.** `{"questions": [ {id, topic, difficulty, tags, question, answer, quiz:{choices[4],
correctIndex}} ]}` — 25 questions each for AI, Machine Learning, Data Science, Data Analytics.

```text
Write a high-quality interview-prep question bank as a single JSON file …
- 100 questions total, 25 in each topic: AI, Machine Learning, Data Science, Data Analytics.
- REAL, technically-correct interview questions & answers (fundamentals, math/stats, algorithms,
  system design, coding, applied, behavioral-technical).
- Difficulty ~40% easy / 40% medium / 20% hard per topic.
Schema per item: id (q001..q100), topic, difficulty, tags[2-4], question,
  answer (80-200 words, markdown), quiz{choices[4], correctIndex}.
Rules: valid quiz on every item; accurate answers; no near-duplicates; output valid JSON only.
```

## 2. Markdown ingestion → flashcard (runtime, optional)

**Where.** `backend/ingest.py → _llm_card()`. Runs only if `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is set. Model:
`claude-haiku-4-5` (cheap, fast — good for bulk note conversion).

**Why.** A user's raw book/notes section is prose, not a clean interview card. This prompt rewrites
one heading section into a crisp question + answer + quiz so it flows through the study pipeline.

**Produces.** Minified JSON `{question, answer (60-160 words md), quiz:{choices[4], correctIndex}}`.

```text
You convert study notes into a single interview flashcard.
Return ONLY minified JSON with keys: question (string),
answer (string, markdown, 60-160 words),
quiz (object with "choices": 4 strings and "correctIndex": int 0-3).
Topic: {topic}. Section heading: {heading}.

Notes:
{section body, truncated to 2000 chars}
```

**Fallback (no key).** `_deterministic_card()` — no LLM. Question = the heading (`Explain: …`),
answer = the section body, tags = keywords from the heading. Always works offline; Quiz mode simply
skips cards that have no `quiz` object.

## Prompt-authoring conventions

Following the workspace prompt template style:
- **Role first** ("You convert…"), then explicit output contract, then the input block last.
- **Hard output format** — "Return ONLY minified JSON" prevents chatty wrappers we'd have to strip.
- **Bounded length** ("60-160 words") keeps cards scannable and costs predictable.
- **Graceful degradation** — every LLM path has a deterministic fallback so the feature never hard-fails.
